destinations.evidence: The consensus block verification pipeline invokes SPC verification against the parent state after settlement verification.
SPC verification reads AsyncJobTracker job data and TEE executor keys from parent state and rejects missing jobs, missing keys, and invalid signatures.

equivalence_argument: The port verifier binds SPC calls to parent-state AsyncJobTracker fields and TEE registry executor data, with explicit rejection on missing data, block/input mismatch, short proof, signature error, or invalid signature.
notes: Mapped to verify_spc_calls in the port block-verification crate.
rationale: The primary diff adds read_jobs_for_spc_verification for AsyncJobTracker storage reads and implements verify_spc_calls to use parent state, read jobs and executor keys, enforce commit-block equality, and return errors for missing data or failed signatures. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: During block verification, fulfilled HTTP/LLM SPC calls must be verified by reading the corresponding AsyncJobTracker job data and ExecutorRegistry public keys from the parent block state, and any missing job, missing executor key, commit-block mismatch, or invalid signature must reject the block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build a block containing a fulfilled HTTP/LLM SPC call and run block verification against parent state variants: missing job, missing executor key, mismatched job commitBlock, invalid proof, and valid proof; assert all invalid variants return BlockVerificationError and only the valid variant succeeds.
summary: Consensus SPC verification uses parent-state job and executor registry data and rejects invalid variants.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Vary parent state for a fulfilled SPC settlement across missing job, missing executor, mismatched commit block/input, short proof, invalid proof, and valid proof; assert only the valid case succeeds.
title: Consensus SPC verification uses parent contract state
